# Dependencies — Line 1z: Total wages

**Tax year:** 2025
**Form:** 1040 / 1040-SR line 1z
**IRS label:** "Add lines 1a through 1h"

Line 1z is a pure arithmetic subtotal — eight `addNonNull` operands aggregating the earned-income block lines 1a through 1h. **Line 1i is EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED** per the IRS line-1i instruction: *"Don't include the amount on line 1i in your total income (line 9) or wages (line 1z)."* Line 1z carries SIGNED amounts (line 1f may be negative under IRC §137(a)(3) special-needs adoption), and bridges per-source wage computations into the Form 1040 total-income flow via line 9. The `hasEmploymentFlag` zero-override ensures `income.totalWages` is non-null whenever `income.wages` is non-null.

---

## Outputs Produced

| Output | JSON path | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `totalWages` | `form1040.income.totalWages` | BigDecimal; null when all 8 sub-lines null AND `hasEmploymentFlag = false`; `BigDecimal.ZERO` when `hasEmploymentFlag = true` with no W-2 inputs |

Set in `buildIncome()` → `Income.setTotalWages(line1z)` at lines 7480-7500.

Line 1z has NO attached form / schedule — single subtotal cell on Form 1040.

---

## Computed Inputs (Eight Sub-Lines)

| Sub-line | Source compute helper | Output model field |
|---|---|---|
| `1a` — Wages, salaries, tips (W-2 box 1 + Form 4852) | `computeLine1aWages` | `income.wages` |
| `1b` — Household employee wages not on W-2 | `sumHouseholdEmployeeWages` / `householdEmployeeAmount` | `income.householdEmployeeWages` |
| `1c` — Tip income not reported on line 1a (Form 4137) | `computeTipIncome` | `income.tipIncome` |
| `1d` — Medicaid waiver payments (Notice 2014-7) | `computeMedicaidWaiverPayments` | `income.medicaidWaiverPayments` |
| `1e` — Taxable dependent care benefits (Form 2441 line 26) | `computeDependentCareBenefits` | `income.dependentCareBenefits` |
| `1f` — Employer-provided adoption benefits (Form 8839 line 31; SIGNED) | `computeAdoptionBenefits` | `income.adoptionBenefits` |
| `1g` — Wages from Form 8919 line 6 | `computeUncollectedSSTax` | `income.uncollectedSocialSecurityMedicareWages` |
| `1h` — Other earned income | `computeOtherEarnedIncome` | `income.otherEarnedIncome` |

---

## Top-Level Formula

```
line1z = roundMoney( addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(
                       line1a, line1b), line1c), line1d), line1e), line1f), line1g), line1h) )

line9  = roundMoney( addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(addNonNull(
                       line1z, line2b), line3b), line4b), line5b), line6b), line7a), line8) )
```

Backend code at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:7361-7363` (exactly 8 operands; lock-in tests protect the operand count).

Each sub-line helper applies `roundMoney()` before returning, so line 1z sums already-rounded values. The `roundMoney(totalWagesRaw)` final wrap is typically a no-op (aggregate is already integral) and a null-passthrough otherwise. Single rounding constant: `ROUNDING_MODE` = WHOLE_DOLLAR HALF_UP at `TaxReturnComputeService.java:111`.

---

## Excluded Items (Critical)

### Line 1i (nontaxable combat pay election) — EXCLUDED

| Reason | Effect of including line 1i |
|---|---|
| Combat pay (W-2 code Q) is excluded from W-2 box 1 under IRC §112 — never enters wages in the first place | Would erroneously increase total wages (already-excluded income reappearing) |
| Line 1i is a CREDIT-WORKSHEET earned-income marker, not a wage subtotal line | Would erroneously increase line 9, AGI, taxable income |
| Including line 1i would defeat the purpose of the election (grant EIC/ACTC access without making the pay taxable) | Would over-tax military families |

Line 1i RE-ENTERS only at:
- Schedule 8812 line 18a (`totalWages + nontaxableCombatPayElection`) — ACTC earned income
- EIC line 27a worksheet (`normalEarnedIncome + nontaxableCombatPayElection`)

Line 1i is SET separately on `income` at lines 7477-7479 (`income.setNontaxableCombatPayElection(...)`), NEVER added to line 1z.

### Other exclusions from line 1z

- Interest / dividend lines (2a, 2b, 3a, 3b)
- IRA, pension, Social Security, capital gain lines (4a/b, 5a/b, 6a/b/c/d, 7a/b)
- Schedule 1 income (line 8)

---

## Input Statement Types

Line 1z reads NO statements directly. All statement reads happen at sub-line compute helpers. The table below shows the indirect chain:

| Statement type | Fields used | Sub-line affected |
|---|---|---|
| `w-2` | `wagesTipsOtherCompAmount` (box 1) + `employeeSSN` | 1a |
| `w-2` | `box12Entries[].code == "T"` + `box12Amount` | 1f (adoption benefits) |
| `w-2` | `socialSecurityWagesAmount` (box 3), `socialSecurityTipsAmount` (box 7), RRTA (box 14) | 1g (Form 8919 line 8 base) |
| `w-2` | `box12Entries[].code` in {D, E, F, S, G} + `box12Amount` | 1h (excess deferrals) |
| `form-4852` | Substitute W-2 wages | 1a |
| `1099-r` | `distributionCodes` (3, 8, P), `taxableAmountAmount`, `grossDistributionAmount`, `iraSepSimple`, `recipientTIN` | 1h (disability, corrective) |
| `1099-misc` | `box3IsStrikeBenefits` + `otherIncomeAmount` | 1h (strike) |

---

## Input Personal Forms

| Form ID | Sub-line owner | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `employment-income-taxpayer` | 1a / 1b | No | `hasEmploymentIncome` drives `hasEmploymentFlag` |
| `employment-income-spouse` | 1a / 1b | No | Same — spouse contribution |
| `tip-income-taxpayer` | 1c | No | Form 4137 path |
| `tip-income-spouse` | 1c | No | Form 4137 path (MFJ) |
| `medicaid-waiver-payments-taxpayer` | 1d | No | — |
| `medicaid-waiver-payments-spouse` | 1d | No | — |
| `childcare-expenses` | 1e | No | Form 2441 dependent care |
| `adoption-expenses` | 1f | No | Form 8839 |
| `uncollected-ss-medicare-taxpayer` | 1g | No | Form 8919 |
| `uncollected-ss-medicare-spouse` | 1g | No | Form 8919 (MFJ) |
| `other-earned-income` | 1h | No | Strike / disability / corrective overrides |

Line 1z aggregation reads NO personal forms directly other than `employment-income-{taxpayer,spouse}` for the `hasEmploymentFlag` derivation. All other personal-form reads happen at sub-line compute helpers.

---

## Output to Forms

| Output | Where |
|---|---|
| `form1040.income.totalWages` | Form 1040 line 1z cell |

No attached form / schedule / PDF appended page / statement attachment. Line 1z is a single arithmetic subtotal.

---

## `hasEmploymentFlag` Zero-Override Invariant

`hasEmploymentFlag` is derived inline at lines 7349-7350 from either spouse's `employment-income-{taxpayer,spouse}.hasEmploymentIncome == true`.

Two mirror behaviors:

1. When `hasEmploymentFlag == true` AND `line 1a == null` → force `income.wages = BigDecimal.ZERO` (lines 7449-7451) so the UI renders `$0` rather than blank on a "yes, expect employment" return with no W-2 yet
2. When `hasEmploymentFlag == true` AND `line1z == null` → force `income.totalWages = BigDecimal.ZERO` (lines 7496-7500)

```java
if (line1z != null) {
    income.setTotalWages(line1z);
} else if (hasEmploymentFlag) {
    income.setTotalWages(BigDecimal.ZERO);
}
```

This enforces: `income.totalWages` is non-null whenever `income.wages` is non-null. Pre-2026-05-10, the backend left `income.totalWages = null` in the flag-true-but-no-W-2 case, relying on a frontend fallback `totalWages ?? wages` for PDF rendering. The fallback has been REMOVED; the invariant is enforced at the source.

Lock-in test `line1zStoresZeroWhenHasEmploymentFlagAndNoWageInputs` protects the override.

**Practical limitation.** The end-to-end "all 8 sub-lines null → line 1z null" path is currently UNREACHABLE in observable scenarios because of a cross-sub-line stylistic 0-vs-null choice — line 1e (`computeDependentCareBenefits`) returns `BigDecimal.ZERO` (not null) when the childcare-expenses form is absent. The defensive `else if (hasEmploymentFlag)` branch is therefore dormant but future-proofs the invariant.

---

## MFS Attribution — Single-Guard Cascade

Line 1z aggregation has NO spouse-attribution logic of its own. Each sub-line owns its MFS gating:

- Lines 1c, 1d, 1e, 1f, 1g, 1h, 1i all accept an `isMfsReturn` parameter (single-guard cascade — 7-line pattern as of 1i Issue #1 closure 2026-05-10)
- Lines 1a/1b use SSN-filter inside `computeLine1aWages` / `sumHouseholdEmployeeWages`

Adding an MFS guard at line 1z would be:
- redundant (sub-lines already gate spouse contributions)
- potentially harmful (could mask sub-line bugs by double-suppressing legitimate values)

Architecturally, the aggregator is the WRONG layer for MFS logic — its job is summing, not filtering.

---

## Validation Flags

**Line 1z emits NO flags.** Line 1z is a pure aggregation; it never blocks the return. Sub-line blocking flags propagate FROM the sub-line computations.

| Sub-line flag (representative) | From sub-line |
|---|---|
| `MISSING_W2_EMPLOYMENT_INCOME_TAXPAYER` | 1a |
| `FORM8919_IRS_DATE_REQUIRED_*` | 1g |
| `ADOPTION_BENEFITS_MFS_EXCLUSION_DISALLOWED` | 1f |
| `LINE1H_DISABILITY_OWNER_REQUIRED` | 1h |
| `LINE1H_DISABILITY_MIN_RETIREMENT_AGE_REQUIRED` | 1h |
| `LINE1H_CORRECTIVE_OWNER_REQUIRED` | 1h |

Scenarios where line 1z does NOT emit a flag despite anomalous values:

| Scenario considered | Why no flag at line 1z | Where blocking actually happens |
|---|---|---|
| Negative line 1z (e.g., from negative line 1f) | Permitted per IRC §137(a)(3) | No flag; `max(0, ...)` floor at line 15 |
| Line 1z exceeds plausible wage range | No invariant; high earners can legitimately exceed $1M | No flag |
| All sub-lines null → line 1z null | Expected | No flag; PDF cell blank |
| `hasEmploymentFlag=true` but no W-2 | Sub-line responsibility | `MISSING_W2_EMPLOYMENT_INCOME_TAXPAYER` from line 1a; line 1z stores `BigDecimal.ZERO` via override |
| Line 1f negative + special-needs gate fails | Sub-line responsibility | Per-child flags from line 1f |

Line 1z is UNIQUE among the 1a–1h–1i set in emitting nothing — every other line has at least one blocking or advisory flag. This is intentional — line 1z is a passive sum.

---

## Compute Order

| Step | Operation |
|---|---|
| 1 | Load all W-2 statement entries; load all personal forms |
| 2 | `computeWages()` → `line1a`, `line1b` |
| 3 | `computeTipIncome()` → `line1c` (also produces `form4137.line10` for 1g) |
| 4 | `computeMedicaidWaiver()` → `line1d` |
| 5 | `computeDependentCareBenefits()` → `line1e` |
| 6 | `computeAdoptionBenefits()` → `line1f` (signed) |
| 7 | `computeUncollectedSSTax(...)` → `line1g` (consumes `form4137.line10`) |
| 8 | `computeOtherEarnedIncome()` → `line1h` |
| 9 | `computeCombatPay()` → `line1i` (stored separately; NOT added to line 1z) |
| 10 | `buildIncome()` at line 7361 — sum 1a–1h → `line1z` via `addNonNull` chain; apply `hasEmploymentFlag` zero-override |
| 11 | Line 9 computed from `line1z + line2b + line3b + line4b + line5b + line6b + line7a + line8` at line 7440 |
| 12 | `computeSchedule8812()` reads `totalWages` + `nontaxableCombatPayElection` (line 1i RE-ENTERS at 18a) |
| 13 | `computeLine27aEIC()` reads `income.getNontaxableCombatPayElection()` (line 1i RE-ENTERS at EIC) |

---

## Key Invariants

- `Line1z = addNonNull(line1a, line1b, line1c, line1d, line1e, line1f, line1g, line1h)` — exactly 8 operands
- `Line1z` MUST NOT include `line 1i` (combat pay election)
- If a component line is negative and valid under IRS rules (line 1f from IRC §137(a)(3)), line 1z must include the negative amount — NO `max(0, ...)` floor at line 1z, 9, or AGI
- `max(0, ...)` floor applied only at line 15 (taxable income)
- If all 8 component lines are null AND `hasEmploymentFlag = false`, line 1z is null and `income.totalWages` is unset
- If `hasEmploymentFlag = true`, `income.totalWages` is non-null (either the computed sum or `BigDecimal.ZERO`)
- `income.totalWages` is non-null whenever `income.wages` is non-null (zero-override mirror invariant)
- Line 1z is the FIRST operand of line 9; null/zero/positive/negative all flow correctly
- ZERO `ReferenceData` constants consumed at line 1z itself — all constants relevant to line 1z arrive pre-applied via the sub-line computations

Lock-in tests:
- `computesLine1zSumExcludesCombatPayElection` — composite scenario: `line 1z = -$5,280` with adoption + combat pay
- `line1zMinimalLine1iExclusion` — minimal: W-2 $4,000 + code Q $2,000 + elect=true → `line 1z = $4,000`, `line 1i = $2,000`
- `line1zEqualsLine1aWhenOnlyW2Wages` — single-sub-line baseline
- `line9EqualsLine1zPlusOtherIncomeLines` — line 9 wiring
- `line1zStoresZeroWhenHasEmploymentFlagAndNoWageInputs` — zero-override
- `schedule8812Line18aSeparatesTotalWagesAndCombatPayDisclosure` — Schedule 8812 separation

---

## Downstream Consumers

| Consumer | How | Where wired |
|---|---|---|
| Form 1040 line 9 (total income) | `line9 = line1z + line2b + line3b + line4b + line5b + line6b + line7a + line8` | TaxReturnComputeService.java:7440-7442 |
| Form 1040 line 11b (AGI) | Indirect: `AGI = line9 − line10` | `buildForm1040` AGI section |
| Form 1040 line 15 (taxable income) | Indirect: `line15 = max(0, AGI − total deductions)` — floor applied here, not at 1z | `buildForm1040` |
| Schedule 8812 line 18a (ACTC earned income) | `line18a = totalWages + nontaxableCombatPayElection`; line 18b discloses the combat-pay component | `computeSchedule8812` lines 19087-19096 |
| Schedule 8812 line 19 (ACTC floor) | `line19 = max(0, line18a − $2,500)` | `computeSchedule8812` |
| EIC line 27a worksheet | EIC earned income includes line 1z component; combat pay added via single-source `income.nontaxableCombatPayElection` | `computeLine27aEIC` |
| Dependent worksheet earned income | `line1z + Schedule 1 line 3 + line 6 + line 8r + line 8t + line 8u` | `computeDependentWorksheetEarnedIncome` at line ~5887 |
| Pub. 915 SS Worksheet 1 line 3 | Includes line 1z reference | `computeSocialSecurityTaxablePortion` at lines ~14807-14848 |

---

## Negative / Null Contract

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `null` | All 8 sub-lines null AND `hasEmploymentFlag = false` — rare in practice (see practical limitation above) |
| `BigDecimal.ZERO` | Either sub-lines aggregate to 0, OR `hasEmploymentFlag = true` with no W-2 inputs |
| Positive | Standard case |
| Negative | Special-needs adoption (line 1f negative) per IRC §137(a)(3); reduces total wages |

---

## Out of Scope

- MFS guard at line 1z level (architecturally wrong layer — sub-lines own MFS gating)
- Frontend fallback `totalWages ?? wages` (REMOVED 2026-05-10; invariant enforced at backend via zero-override)
- 2024 line spec treating line 1z as "1a through 1i" (line 1i is EXCLUDED for 2025)
